channel.title: Observability on Validated Patterns
channel.description: Recent content in Observability on Validated Patterns

item.title: MLOps Fraud Detection
item.description: This pattern demonstrates how Red Hat OpenShift AI can be used to build an end-to-end MLOps platform. It demonstrates this using a credit card fraud detection use case.

item.title: Telco Hub
item.description: The Telco Hub pattern is a GitOps-first validated pattern for deploying and operating a Telco-focused management hub.

item.title: OPEA Chat QnA accelerated with AMD Instinct
item.description: This pattern aids with deployment of OPEA’s Chat QnA RAG application, accelerated with AMD Instinct hardware.

item.title: Federated Edge Observability
item.description: This pattern uses OpenShift Virtualization to simulate an edge environment for VMs which then report metrics via OpenTelemetry.

item.title: Ingress Mesh BGP
item.description: This pattern demonstrates multi-cluster service mesh networking using MetalLB with BGP and Red Hat Service Interconnect (Skupper) for cross-cluster connectivity.

item.title: TravelOps
item.description: This pattern enables you to secure your applications using Red Hat Service Mesh.

item.title: Emerging Disease Detection
item.description: This pattern is based on a demo implementation of an automated data pipeline for sepsis analysis previously developed by Red Hat.

item.title: Connected Vehicle Architecture
item.description: A distributed cloud-native application that implements key aspects of a modern IoT architecture.

item.title: Kong
item.description: A pattern for Kong Gateway Control Plane and Data Plane demo.

item.title: Multicluster DevSecOps
item.description: This pattern is derived from the multi-cloud GitOps pattern with added products to provide a complete DevSecOps workflow.

item.title: Industrial Edge
item.description: This pattern demonstrates a horizontal solution for Industrial Edge use cases.
